description: SKILL.md frontmatter description is 240 chars, exceeds the 130-char skill limit per skill_md_template.md §2 'Frontmatter Requirements' (line ~75: 'Single line, ≤130 chars for skills'). Long descriptions risk auto-discovery truncation when the 5,600-char workspace budget is approached.
recommended_fix: Trim description to ≤130 chars. Suggested: 'Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.'

description: SKILL.md missing the recommended `<!-- Keywords: ... -->` HTML comment block after frontmatter (skill_md_template.md §3 Template line 128). Keywords aid discoverability indexing.
recommended_fix: Add `<!-- Keywords: deep-loop-runtime, executor, coverage-graph, bayesian-scorer, prompt-pack, fallback-router, jsonl-repair, loop-lock, council -->` between frontmatter close and H1 title.

description: SKILL.md §8 REFERENCES line 266 cites `changelog/v1.0.0.md` but actual file is `changelog/v1.0.0.0.md` (four-segment semver). Same broken ref appears in README.md (AF-0010). Class-of-bug across multiple consumers; class scope includes any inline link to the changelog.
recommended_fix: Replace `changelog/v1.0.0.md` with `changelog/v1.0.0.0.md` in SKILL.md §8 and README.md §6 RELATED DOCUMENTS table; also update internal `[changelog/v1.0.0.md]` markdown link.

description: `## Council Primitives` (line 142) is a top-level H2 inside Section 3 HOW IT WORKS but breaks the 'numbered H2 sections (ALL CAPS)' rule from skill_md_template.md §1.6. It is neither a numbered section nor demoted to H3 sub-section under §3.
recommended_fix: Demote to `### Council Primitives` as an H3 sub-section of §3 HOW IT WORKS. Adjust subsequent content levels accordingly.

description: RULES section (§4) uses `**ALWAYS**`, `**NEVER**`, `**ESCALATE**` bold-text labels. The sk-doc skill_md_template.md prescribes ✅ ALWAYS / ❌ NEVER / ⚠️ ESCALATE IF with emoji prefixes (§3 'Required Sections' line ~112).
recommended_fix: Either add the ✅ / ❌ / ⚠️ emoji prefixes per template OR record explicit deviation in audit-summary.md (consistent with overall project emoji policy from HVR which allows max-1-emoji per piece — current SKILL.md uses zero).

description: SKILL.md §1 WHEN TO USE has 'Activation Triggers' + 'When NOT to Use' but is missing the 'Use Cases' sub-section with [Primary Use Case Category] + [Secondary Use Case Category] specified by skill_md_template.md §3 Template (lines 154-162). Justifiable deviation: this is a non-user-facing runtime infrastructure skill with no operator modes.
recommended_fix: Either add a 'Use Cases' sub-section enumerating the runtime call paths (workflow YAML invocation, TS import, operator script invocation) OR record explicit deviation rationale in audit-summary.md ('runtime infrastructure skill — no user-facing modes').

description: SKILL.md is 266 LOC. The sk-doc template recommends 800-2000 LOC for skills with bundled resources (skill_md_template.md §3 'Target'). Compact authoring is reasonable for a runtime infrastructure skill with no user-facing modes, but warrants Phase 3 reassessment in light of the README rewrite expansion.
recommended_fix: During Phase 3 README rewrite, assess whether HOW IT WORKS or ARCHITECTURE sections need expansion to mirror README depth. If kept compact, document deviation rationale in audit-summary.md.

description: README.md §6 RELATED DOCUMENTS line 167 cites `changelog/v1.0.0.md` but actual file is `changelog/v1.0.0.0.md`. Sibling of AF-0003.
recommended_fix: Update path to `changelog/v1.0.0.0.md`. Will be addressed during Phase 3 full rewrite.

description: README.md is missing CONFIGURATION, USAGE EXAMPLES, TROUBLESHOOTING, FAQ sections that the system-spec-kit README structural anchor (9-section pattern) prescribes. Current README has only OVERVIEW + QUICK START + FEATURES + STRUCTURE + INTEGRATION + RELATED. Will be addressed by Phase 3 full rewrite (per plan).
recommended_fix: Phase 3 rewrite adds the 4 missing sections per the 9-section structural anchor.
deferral_rationale: Deferred to Phase 3 full README rewrite per plan.md §Phase 3.

description: README.md frontmatter has title, description, trigger_phrases but is missing `importance_tier` and `contextType` fields that are standard across this repo's skill READMEs (e.g. system-spec-kit/README.md, sk-doc/README.md). Aids skill-advisor scoring and graph metadata derivation.
recommended_fix: Add `importance_tier: "important"` and `contextType: "general"` (or "reference") to frontmatter. Will be addressed during Phase 3 rewrite.
deferral_rationale: Deferred to Phase 3 full README rewrite per plan.md §Phase 3.

description: README.md is 174 LOC vs the system-spec-kit/README.md structural anchor at 1053 LOC. Tone is purely descriptive; lacks the marketing-leaning HVR voice anchored at ~70% of Public/README.md intensity that Phase 3 calls for.
recommended_fix: Phase 3 rewrite addresses tone + depth + 9-section structure.
deferral_rationale: Deferred to Phase 3 full README rewrite per plan.md §Phase 3.

description: references/coverage_graph_schema.md frontmatter is conformant (title, description, trigger_phrases, importance_tier, contextType) per sk-doc skill_reference_template.md. Spot-check passed. Class-of-bug check: verify remaining 3 references/*.md files have same conformant frontmatter.
recommended_fix: No fix needed for spot-checked file. Phase 2 dispatch (if dispatched) confirms class. Otherwise verify via `head -15` on the other 3 references/*.md.

description: feature_catalog.md root index claims '17 entries' (line 31) but the 7 domain folders together contain 18 .md files (3+1+1+4+1+3+4=17 — actually correct, 17 not 18 since I miscounted earlier; review confirms 17 per-feature files + 1 index = 18 files total). The 'Coverage' column in the catalog index sums to 3+1+1+4+1+3+4=17 features ✓. Class-of-bug check: per-feature file counts match folder names.
recommended_fix: No fix needed — count is accurate (17 features + 1 index = 18 files). Marked resolved.

description: Spot-check sample of feature_catalog per-feature files (01-executor-config.md, etc.) shows uniform 57-58 LOC entries. Per `feature_catalog_creation.md` standards, per-feature files should have Description + Current Reality + Source Files + Status sub-sections. Verify all 17 entries follow this structure (one spot-check; class-of-bug if any deviate).
recommended_fix: Phase 5 deep-research loop verifies the full class; if non-conformance found, escalate as P1.
deferral_rationale: Class-of-bug confirmation deferred to Phase 5 deep-research iteration on feature_catalog conformance.

description: Manual testing playbook root index follows sk-doc playbook pattern (EXECUTION POLICY block, 14-section TOC including REVIEW PROTOCOL + DETERMINISTIC COMMAND NOTATION). Per-feature files spot-check shows uniform 83 LOC. Class-of-bug check: per-feature files have Objective + Prompt + Execution Steps + Source Anchors + Verdict criteria as required by playbook_creation.md.
recommended_fix: Phase 5 deep-research loop verifies full class conformance; spot-check passes.
deferral_rationale: Class-of-bug confirmation deferred to Phase 5 deep-research iteration on playbook conformance.

description: Sub-READMEs (lib/, lib/council/, lib/coverage-graph/, lib/deep-loop/, scripts/, tests/, tests/helpers/, storage/) have no sk-doc template — they are sub-component orientation docs for code subdirectories. Per ADR-003 logic (absent-by-design rationale applies analogously), these are documented deviations from a strict 1:1 sk-doc template mapping. All 8 spot-checked at LOC range 28-47 (terse, purposeful).
recommended_fix: Log as documented deviation in Phase 4 validation-report.md with `no-template:sub-readme` classification. No action required.
deferral_rationale: No applicable sk-doc template; phase-4 validation logs deviation per artifact_type 'sub_readme' with 'no-template' note.

description: changelog/v1.0.0.0.md has expected sections (Why This Release Matters + New Features + Files Changed + Upgrade + References) but does NOT carry frontmatter with `version` + `release_date` + other fields the changelog-entry.schema.json may require. The schema validates JSON-shape changelog entries; the .md file may use a different convention. Verify against schema.
recommended_fix: During Phase 3 (when authoring v1.1.0.0.md), check whether changelog-entry.schema.json applies to .md frontmatter or only to a separate JSON sidecar. If sidecar-only, no action; if .md frontmatter is required, retroactive frontmatter addition is out-of-scope (defer to follow-on packet).
deferral_rationale: Schema applicability ambiguous; clarify during Phase 3 authoring of v1.1.0.0.md.

description: LOG_ONLY pass on lib/**/ code (13 TS/CJS modules, ~4,000 LOC): high-level review only per ADR-004 no-code-edit boundary. Modules carry MODULE: headers per OpenCode TS-MODULE-HEADER convention (referenced in SKILL.md §4 ALWAYS rules). No defects spotted in spot-check of executor-config.ts (275 LOC) and bayesian-scorer.ts (44 LOC). Phase 5 deep-research loop may surface deeper logic gaps.
evidence: SKILL.md:189 ALWAYS rule for MODULE: header
recommended_fix: None in this packet — code findings deferred to follow-on packets per ADR-004 boundary.
deferral_rationale: ADR-004 no-code-edit boundary; defer all code-class findings to follow-on packets.

description: LOG_ONLY pass on scripts/ (4 entry-point .cjs + 1 guard lib, ~1,103 LOC): per SKILL.md §4 ALWAYS rules, scripts must (1) include `'use strict';` on line 2, (2) emit JSON-only output to stdout, (3) use try/finally around DB open/close. Spot-check expected to confirm; verify in Phase 5 deep-research.
recommended_fix: None in this packet per ADR-004. Phase 5 deep-research loop verifies invariants if any logic gap surfaces.

description: LOG_ONLY pass on tests/ (22 vitest files, 1 spawn-cjs helper, ~3,651 LOC): per CHANGELOG line 43, integration tests reference 'Phase B review-depth fixtures' (3 files). Tests are runtime-owned and discovered via the system-spec-kit MCP server vitest.config.ts glob `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'`.
recommended_fix: None in this packet per ADR-004. No defects spotted at high-level review.

description: Skill-level graph-metadata.json (170 LOC) provides peer-skill discoverability metadata (trigger_phrases, key_topics, entities, dependencies, related_to). Not edited unless cascade forces it per ADR-007 guard.
recommended_fix: No fix needed. If touched (e.g. to add new related_to entry post-rewrite), re-run `skill_graph_compiler.py --export-json --pretty` per `feedback_skill_graph_compiler_rebuild` memory.
